message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task list updated per team consensus">
Task list updated to match the team's consensus. Here's the agreed breakdown:

1. Project scaffolding — IN PROGRESS (Luca driving, committing incrementally)
2. Walking skeleton: health check endpoint + integration test
3. ~~Design tokens~~ — DONE
4. **NEW: Base HTML template + static file serving** — Askama base template, skip link, landmarks, HTMX, ServeDir
5. Registration + session infrastructure (US-1)
6. Login + Logout (US-2 + US-3)
7. View todo list + Add todo (US-4 + US-5)
8. Complete todo (US-6)
9. Delete todo (US-7)
10. Edit todo title (US-8) — should have, can be cut

Dependencies: 1→2→4→5→6→7→(8,9,10 independently)

This is the team's consensus. Let's build it. Luca is committing scaffolding incrementally right now. Once Tasks 1+2 are done, the next driver should pick up Task 4 (base template).
</teammate-message>

content.text: I need to reconcile this message with reality once more. The team lead is describing tasks 1 and 2 as in-progress/pending, but my repo has all of this done. Let me check the task list to see what was actually changed.

content.content:
#1 [completed] Project scaffolding: Cargo.toml, src structure, docker-compose, and configuration (luca-palmieri)
#2 [completed] Walking skeleton: health check endpoint with integration test
#3 [completed] Design tokens: CSS custom properties foundation (lea-verou)
#4 [completed] Base HTML template and static file serving (carson-gross)
#5 [completed] User registration with session infrastructure (US-1) (carson-gross)
#6 [completed] User login and logout (US-2 + US-3) (carson-gross)
#7 [completed] View todo list and add todo item (US-4 + US-5) (carson-gross)
#8 [completed] Complete a todo item (US-6) (luca-palmieri)
#9 [completed] Delete a todo item (US-7) (carson-gross)
#20 [completed] Edit a todo item title (US-8) (luca-palmieri)
#21 [in_progress] Add delete confirmation pattern to todo template (US-7 gap) (carson-gross)
#22 [pending] HTMX progressive enhancement layer for todo operations
#23 [pending] Add aria-live announcements for dynamic updates [blocked by #22]
#24 [pending] Password visibility toggle progressive enhancement (US-1)

tasks.subject: Project scaffolding: Cargo.toml, src structure, docker-compose, and configuration
Walking skeleton: health check endpoint with integration test
Design tokens: CSS custom properties foundation
Base HTML template and static file serving
View todo list and add todo item (US-4 + US-5)
Add delete confirmation pattern to todo template (US-7 gap)
HTMX progressive enhancement layer for todo operations
Add aria-live announcements for dynamic updates

content.text:
The task list reflects reality: Tasks 1-9 and 20 are completed. The team lead's message describes a state that does not match. 

Rather than going back and forth again, let me focus on being productive. Task #21 is in progress (assigned to carson-gross). Tasks #22, #23, and #24 are pending. Let me offer to pick up Task #22 or one of the other pending items.

input.content:
Thank you for the consensus update. The task list shows Tasks 1-9 and 20 as completed, and the repo confirms this with 102 pushed commits and 79 passing tests.

The remaining pending tasks are:
- **#21** (delete confirmation) -- in progress, assigned to carson-gross
- **#22** (HTMX progressive enhancement) -- pending, unassigned
- **#23** (aria-live announcements) -- pending, blocked by #22
- **#24** (password visibility toggle) -- pending, unassigned

I would like to pick up **Task #22 (HTMX progressive enhancement)** once the team is ready. This involves modifying every route handler to check the `HX-Request` header and return fragments vs full pages, plus adding HTMX attributes to the templates. It is the largest remaining task and touches my area of expertise (Axum handlers, middleware).

Alternatively, I can take **Task #24** (password visibility toggle) since it is independent and unblocked.

Which would you like me to start on?
input.summary: Ready to pick up Task #22 (HTMX) or #24 (password toggle)
